svg.font-family: -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
svg.aria-label: The three-tier access structure created by the June 26 Lutnick letter for Anthropic's Covered Models
svg.title: After the June 26 Lutnick letter, the export order is no longer one blanket suspension but a three-tier, permissioned structure: Mythos 5 returns license-free to roughly 100 vetted entities, Mythos 5 stays license-gated for everyone else, and Fable 5 remains fully suspended.
svg.text: From blanket ban to a three-tier carve-out — the “Covered Models”
June 26 Lutnick letter. The Annex A entity list and the technical diversion-risk mitigations are not public.
Tier 1 · Mythos 5 → Annex A entities — RESTORED, no license required
~100 vetted US critical-infrastructure orgs (gov agencies + private firms) and their foreign-national employees;
US government civilian agencies and national labs; Anthropic's own foreign-national employees.
Anthropic restoring access quickly. Commerce reserves the right to revoke or adjust the list.
Tier 2 · Mythos 5 → everyone else — LICENSE REQUIRED
Export / reexport / in-country transfer outside Annex A still needs a license. All June 12 conditions remain,
including the criminal and civil penalties of the original order.
Tier 3 · Fable 5 → all users — STILL FULLY SUSPENDED
The general-purpose flagship most subscribers and developers are waiting for is untouched by the carve-out.
No announced restoration date. The letter covers Mythos only.
Adjusting a license *requirement* on named entities is an Export Administration Regulations lever — a data point
favoring the EAR theory of the ban's basis (vs. IEEPA), and the first concrete restoration mechanism in the saga.
